title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Decision Notebook: Objective Constraints
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int objective constraints increment in an explainable constrained optimization model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
Build transition, network, and resource matrices from domain relationships.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Objective Constraints evidence
steps.instruction: Calculate the determinant of [[3, 1], [2, 6]]. Use the result to decide Objective Constraints for an explainable constrained optimization model.
steps.why: The Objective Constraints result for an explainable constrained optimization model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (3 × 6) − (1 × 2). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
